notes: Adds the in-app multi-tab chip. When PHANTOM is open in a second live tab, a small burnt-orange pill (#phantom-tab-pill, 'N TABS') appears in the NOW header next to the SW status pill — same dimensions/anatomy as .sw-pill, recolored to match the boot-update row, so the multi-tab feature reads as one thing across boot and in-app. Tap shows a 'PHANTOM is open in another tab — close it to avoid data loss' toast. This closes the gap the boot-screen row left: the boot row only warns a newly-opened tab, while the chip warns whichever tab is active (including two tabs both past the boot screen). Liveness is heartbeat-based: live tabs already re-announce every 30s; tabs silent past TAB_TTL (70s) are pruned and the chip refreshed (every 20s + on every beacon), so a closed tab — including iOS Safari tabs that freeze without firing beforeunload — never leaves a stale warning. No floating banners anywhere; chip lives in existing header chrome and never covers content. Clone-war detection (BroadcastChannel + storage fallback) unchanged. Bundles v1.6.39 (clone-war boot row) and v1.6.38 (resume boot row). All three version stamps bumped in lockstep.
